prompt: just did `git pull` on my spyglass clone, now `from spyglass.decoding.v1.sorted_spikes import SortedSpikesDecodingV1` raises `ImportError: cannot import name 'SortedSpikesDecodingV1'`. didn't change anything on my end.
behavioral_checks: Checks `spyglass.__file__` (or `pip show spyglass-neuro` editable status) BEFORE recommending anything destructive — distinguishes Shape A (site-packages shadowing) from Shape B (editable desync after pyproject.toml / setup.cfg bump)
Notes that a `git pull` that bumps `setup.cfg` / `pyproject.toml` can desync a previously editable install; re-running `pip install -e .` from the checkout refreshes the editable wrapper
Does NOT recommend `pip install spyglass-neuro` from PyPI as the fix — that creates the site-packages-shadowing problem if the user already had an editable checkout
Mentions kernel restart for Jupyter when reinstalling — Python caches imports across `pip install` so the running kernel won't pick up the new module otherwise
